skill.name: ppt-editing-skill
skill.description: Edit existing PowerPoint files or templates with XML-safe workflows. Use it to analyze layouts, map content to slides, duplicate or reorder slides, edit slide XML, clean orphaned assets, and repack validated PPTX output.
skill.summary: Edit existing PowerPoint templates through structured XML and PPTX archive workflows.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk matches, but review found no malicious intent, no network access, and no prompt injection attempt. Most high-severity findings are false positives from Markdown backticks, XML examples, placeholder ellipses, and prose. The remaining risk is medium because the skill legitimately instructs users to run local commands and manipulate PPTX files, extracted archives, and temporary files.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution Required for PPTX Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to copy PowerPoint files, run markitdown, and use helper scripts for unpacking, slide duplication, cleanup, and packing. This is expected for PPTX editing, but it can affect local files if paths are wrong or untrusted helper scripts are supplied.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains explicit shell command examples and named helper scripts. The behavior is task-relevant and not malicious, but command execution is a real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Archive and Temporary File Manipulation
medium_findings.description: The skill describes editing unpacked PPTX XML, using Python zipfile behavior, and writing to /tmp before copying to a final path. This is normal for PPTX archives, but it requires careful path handling and cleanup.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow clearly depends on local archive extraction and filesystem writes. No evidence shows traversal or exfiltration, so the concern is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Static Matches Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The external command detector matched many Markdown inline-code backticks and fenced examples. These are documentation markers, not Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown documentation, and the cited backticks delimit code terms or examples. No Ruby source or executable backtick expression is present.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Static Matches Are XML Ellipses
low_findings.description: The path traversal detector matched placeholder ellipses inside XML examples. The cited text is not a filesystem path and does not instruct traversal outside the workspace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious sequence appears only as abbreviated XML attributes in formatting examples. No path join, file read, or parent-directory access is shown.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reviewed skill does not contain cryptographic APIs, hashing code, password handling, or encryption logic. The static matches occur in prose and markup related to presentation editing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operation in the only scanned file. The cited locations describe PowerPoint structure, cleanup, or XML formatting.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell and Helper Script Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow includes shell command examples and references to local helper scripts. Users should run it only in trusted workspaces and with trusted PPTX inputs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and central to the documented workflow. They do not show malicious intent, but they are a concrete execution pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct Filesystem Writes and Temporary Output
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs users to create predictable files, work with an unpacked PPTX tree, and write temporary output before copying the final deck.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is clearly documented and expected for archive editing. The risk depends on path discipline and source file trust.

content.value_statement: Template-based PowerPoint edits can break slide relationships, formatting, and packaged assets. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through XML-safe PPTX editing workflows.
actual_capabilities: Copies a user-provided PPTX template into a predictable working file.
Extracts template text and slide structure with markitdown.
Guides safe slide duplication, deletion, and reordering through presentation XML.
Supports parallel slide XML editing after structural changes are complete.
Cleans orphaned media, relationships, and unused slide assets.
Repackages and validates the edited PPTX output.
limitations: It relies on external helper scripts that must exist in the workspace.
It does not generate presentation design strategy from scratch.
It requires careful manual review for layout overflow and visual alignment.
It should not be used with untrusted PPTX files without sandboxing.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Client Proposal
use_cases.description: Update text, slide order, and repeated sections while preserving the approved PowerPoint template.

use_cases.title: Localize a Training Deck
use_cases.description: Replace placeholder content across many slides while keeping template formatting and slide relationships intact.

use_cases.title: Produce Variant Sales Decks
use_cases.description: Duplicate, remove, and reorder template slides for different prospects without rebuilding the deck manually.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ppt-editing-skill to inspect this PowerPoint template. Summarize the slide layouts, placeholders, and likely content mapping options.

prompt_templates.title: Update Slide Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ppt-editing-skill to replace the placeholders in this PPTX with my supplied content. Preserve the template layout and report any likely overflow.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Custom Deck Variant
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ppt-editing-skill to duplicate, delete, and reorder template slides for this audience. Complete structural changes before editing slide XML.

prompt_templates.title: Repair and Validate a Repacked Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ppt-editing-skill to clean orphaned assets, validate relationships, repack the PPTX, and list any remaining manual QA checks.

output_examples.input: A branded proposal template and new client-specific content.
output_examples.output: An edited PowerPoint deck with updated sections, preserved formatting, and a short QA summary.

output_examples.input: A training deck template with too many repeated exercise slides.
output_examples.output: A trimmed deck with unused slides removed, slide order corrected, and orphaned assets cleaned.

output_examples.input: A sales template that needs separate versions for three customer segments.
output_examples.output: Three deck variants using the same visual system and adjusted slide selections.

best_practices: Work on a copy of the user-provided PowerPoint file.
Finish slide duplication, deletion, and ordering before editing slide content.
Run content extraction and visual QA after edits to catch overflow or mismatched counts.
anti_patterns: Do not manually copy slide files when a helper script handles relationships.
Do not clear unused template text while leaving empty shapes or images behind.
Do not concatenate multiple content items into one paragraph.

faq.question: What kind of PowerPoint work is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for editing existing PPTX templates while preserving layout, relationships, and packaged assets.

faq.question: Can it create a deck without a template?
faq.answer: It is optimized for template-based editing, not full deck design from a blank file.

faq.question: Does it edit slide XML directly?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides direct XML edits after structural slide operations are complete.

faq.question: Why does it use helper scripts?
faq.answer: PPTX files contain many linked XML parts. Helper scripts reduce relationship and packaging errors.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the reviewed skill file.

faq.question: What should users review before delivery?
faq.answer: Users should inspect slide layout, text overflow, missing media, and final PowerPoint opening behavior.
